report_name: Kenyan President's Website Hacked, Attackers Demand Bitcoin Ransom

threat_actors.name: Unknown Attackers of Kenyan Presidential Website
threat_actors.description: The unidentified threat actors responsible for the defacement and ransom demand against the Kenyan President's website in July 2026.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: The attackers likely exploited a vulnerability in the website's content management system (CMS), a plugin, or the underlying web server software to gain initial access.

attack_patterns.name: Internal Defacement
attack_patterns.description: The attackers altered the homepage of the presidential website to display their ransom demand, causing reputational damage.

attack_patterns.name: Web Shell
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely planted a web shell, such as a malicious PHP, JSP, or ASPX file, to maintain persistence and execute commands on the compromised web server.

attack_patterns.name: System Information Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely executed basic reconnaissance commands such as whoami, uname -a, or ipconfig via a web shell to gather information about the compromised server.

tools.name: SQLmap
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool for vulnerability scanning and exploitation of the public-facing website.

tools.name: Nikto
tools.description: Mentioned as a potential tool for vulnerability scanning of the public-facing website.

identities.name: Government of Kenya
identities.description: The owner of the compromised presidential website. The government confirmed the security incident and initiated response protocols.

identities.name: ICT Authority
identities.description: The Kenyan government's ICT authority, which handled the incident response for the presidential website compromise.

identities.name: National Computer and Cybercrime Coordination Committee (NC4)
identities.description: Kenyan government committee that reported on the high volume of cyber threats facing the country.

indicators.name: Potential Web Shell by Filename
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Potential Web Shell 'shell.php' Creation
id: 2b5c1f8a-3e4d-4b1a-8c7d-9e0f1a2b3c4d
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of a file named 'shell.php', which is a common name for a web shell, in a web server directory.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
references:
  - https://thekenyatimes.com/breaking-news/government-issues-statement-after-state-house-website-was-hacked/
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\wwwroot\'
      - '\inetpub\'
    TargetFilename|endswith: '\shell.php'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the creation of a file named 'shell.php', which is a common indicator of a web shell being planted on a server.

indicators.name: Suspicious Script File Creation in Web Root
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious Script File Creation in Web Root
id: 9d8c7b6a-5f4e-4d3c-2b1a-0e9f8d7c6b5a
status: experimental
description: Detects the creation of suspicious script files (php, jsp, aspx) in common web server root directories. This can indicate the placement of a web shell.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
references:
  - https://thekenyatimes.com/breaking-news/government-issues-statement-after-state-house-website-was-hacked/
tags:
  - attack.t1505.003
level: high
logsource:
  category: file_event
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_ext:
    TargetFilename|endswith:
      - '.php'
      - '.jsp'
      - '.aspx'
  selection_path:
    TargetFilename|contains:
      - '\wwwroot\'
      - '\inetpub\'
      - '/var/www/'
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate deployment of new web application files.
indicators.description: Detects the creation of script files like .php, .jsp, or .aspx in web server directories, a common sign of a web shell being planted.

indicators.name: Web Server Process Spawning Reconnaissance Tools
indicators.pattern:
title: Web Server Process Spawning Reconnaissance Tools
id: 1a2b3c4d-5e6f-7a8b-9c0d-1e2f3a4b5c6d
status: experimental
description: Detects a web server process spawning common reconnaissance command-line tools. This is highly indicative of post-exploitation activity via a web shell.
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-18
references:
  - https://thekenyatimes.com/breaking-news/government-issues-statement-after-state-house-website-was-hacked/
tags:
  - attack.t1082
level: high
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_child:
    Image|endswith:
      - '\whoami.exe'
      - '\ipconfig.exe'
      - '\systeminfo.exe'
      - '\net.exe'
      - '\netstat.exe'
  selection_parent:
    ParentImage|endswith:
      - '\w3wp.exe' # IIS
      - '\httpd.exe' # Apache
      - '\nginx.exe' # Nginx
      - '\php-cgi.exe' # PHP
  condition: all of selection_*
falsepositives:
  - Unusual administrative scripts.
indicators.description: Detects a web server user account (e.g., www-data, apache, iusr) executing basic reconnaissance commands, suggesting a web shell is in use.
